title: Deputy School Head, School of International Air Traffic Services
description:
Job Summary

The Deputy School Head, School of ATS – International (“SATS-I”), supports the Head of SATS by leading strategic planning, operational governance, and program development to advance SATS-I’s training excellence and international engagement.

Responsibilities

  • Develop and implement a strategic plan for SATS-I, including identifying training needs, defining target audiences, establishing training policies, and setting marketing plans and targets to drive growth
  • Design operational frameworks and governance structures to ensure effective management and uphold good school practices within SATS-I
  • Conceptualize and develop courses, curricula, syllabi, and structured programs aligned with SATS-I’s strategic objectives to meet the training needs of international audiences
  • Identify and pursue opportunities to promote SATS-I actively, cultivating and strengthening partnerships with regional and international aviation stakeholders
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with the international aviation community to stay informed of emerging training trends and industry developments
  • Collaborate strategically and operationally with internal and external stakeholders to ensure seamless execution of SATS-I initiatives and daily operations
  • Regularly review and evaluate the effectiveness of training programs and instructors, implementing improvements to enhance training quality and outcomes
  • Recommend capital projects and procurement initiatives essential for the operational efficiency and continuous improvement of SATS-I
  • Optimize deployment of staff and resources to maximize operational effectiveness and support organizational goals

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Proven experience leading transformation and strategic projects within organizations, academies, or institutions of higher learning
  • Technical knowledge of civil aviation, advantageous for aligning training with industry standards
  • Demonstrated leadership in fostering high-performing, engaged teams and managing cross-functional collaboration
  • Strong strategic thinking, analytical capabilities, and problem-solving skills applied to complex organizational challenges
  • Excellent interpersonal, verbal, written communication, and presentation skills to influence and engage diverse stakeholders


Location: Airport
Salary: basic salary + AWS
Mondays to Fridays office hours

EA License No: 96C4864
Reg. No.: R24121728 EUNICE WOO JING QI
